skill.name: behavioral-modes
skill.description: Adaptive AI behavior modes for brainstorming, implementation, debugging, review, teaching, release preparation, and multi-agent planning.
skill.summary: Switch AI assistants between task-specific operating modes for clearer responses and better workflow fit.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline backticks, emoji-rich examples, and substring matches in ordinary text; no executable code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts are false positives. The marked locations are Markdown fenced examples or inline formatting, not executable Ruby code or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is plain Markdown and the cited regions are examples or inline formatting. The skill frontmatter only allows Read, Glob, and Grep, so no shell execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Substring Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts are false positives. The cited lines contain names, headings, and descriptive text, not cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, or key handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptography-related operation in the file. The alerts align with ordinary prose and mode names rather than security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is an Output Template
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert is a false positive. The cited line is part of a debugging response template and does not request host, process, network, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line belongs to an example response format for prevention guidance. No command, file access, network probe, or system inventory instruction appears there.

content.user_title: Switch AI Workflows by Task Mode
content.value_statement: General AI responses can be too broad for specialized work. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code clear modes for planning, coding, debugging, review, teaching, and release readiness.
actual_capabilities: Defines seven task-oriented response modes for common software workflows.
Maps user trigger phrases to suitable modes such as brainstorm, implement, debug, review, teach, and ship.
Provides response format examples for planning, troubleshooting, review, explanation, and release checks.
Adds guidance for plan-execute-critic collaboration on complex tasks.
Supports explicit manual mode switching with slash-style user requests.
limitations: It provides prompt guidance only and does not execute commands or automate tools.
Mode selection depends on the assistant correctly interpreting the user request.
It references a clean-code skill but does not include that separate skill.
It does not define project-specific quality gates or deployment rules.

use_cases.title: Plan Product Features
use_cases.description: Use brainstorm mode to compare several product or architecture options before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Debug Application Issues
use_cases.description: Use debug mode to ask for symptoms, test hypotheses, explain root cause, and propose prevention steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release
use_cases.description: Use ship mode to focus on stability, security checks, test status, and release readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request when the user wants the assistant to infer the best behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use behavioral-modes. Select the best mode for this task, explain the choice briefly, then help me with: [task].

prompt_templates.title: Brainstorm Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning request before code or implementation decisions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use brainstorm mode for [feature or problem]. Give at least three approaches with pros, cons, and a recommended next step.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Troubleshooting request with symptoms and logs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use debug mode. Investigate this issue, identify likely root causes, propose tests, and give the safest fix: [symptoms and context].

prompt_templates.title: Run Plan Execute Critic
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for complex engineering changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use plan-execute-critic mode for [complex task]. Create a plan, implement the first safe step, then review the result for risks.

output_examples.input: Brainstorm ways to improve onboarding for a SaaS dashboard.
output_examples.output: The assistant presents three clear approaches, compares benefits and tradeoffs, and asks which direction should move into implementation.

output_examples.input: Debug why login fails after a deployment.
output_examples.output: The assistant summarizes the symptom, lists likely causes, recommends targeted checks, explains the root cause, and gives prevention steps.

output_examples.input: Review this pull request before release.
output_examples.output: The assistant groups findings by severity, explains each risk, suggests concise fixes, and ends with release readiness notes.

best_practices: Name the desired mode when the task has a clear goal.
Provide context such as logs, requirements, code area, or release constraints.
Switch modes as the work moves from planning to implementation or review.
anti_patterns: Do not use implement mode when requirements are still unclear.
Do not treat ship mode as a substitute for project-specific release checks.
Do not expect the skill to run tests or inspect files without normal tool access.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It defines task-specific behavior modes so an AI assistant can answer in a style that fits the work.

faq.question: Does it execute shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown instruction file and only declares Read, Glob, and Grep as allowed tools.

faq.question: Can I force a specific mode?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes manual mode switching examples such as brainstorm, implement, debug, and review.

faq.question: Is this only for coding?
faq.answer: No. It is strongest for software work, but teach and brainstorm modes can support general planning and explanation.

faq.question: Does it include the clean-code skill?
faq.answer: No. It references clean-code standards, but that skill is not included in this package.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
